title: Use XDG_CONFIG_HOME for ~/.config/containers/certs.d ?
body:
### Issue Description

if XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set to a directory which is owned by the user and HOME is also set, then $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/.config/containers/certs.d/* certificates are not taken into account. Only $HOME/.config/containers/certs.d/* are being checked and as a last resort /etc/containers/certs.d/* certificates are checked.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. export XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/whatever/writable/path
2. put certificate symlink to my_registry under $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/.config/containers/certs.d/
3. execute podman pull <any_image_from_a_registry_which_has_a_cert_from_above_path>


### Describe the results you received

$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/.config/containers/certs.d/my_registry is not taken into account. 
$HOME/.config/containers/certs.d/my_registry is being checked instead.

### Describe the results you expected

$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/.config/containers/certs.d/my_registry has priority over $HOME/.config/containers/certs.d/my_registry

### podman info output

```yaml
podman version 5.2.2
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
The containers-certs.d(5) man page does not document XDG_CONFIG_HOME support for the certs.d dir so this is "expected".

The lookup is done by c/image so I move the issue there. Other config files there have the same issue, compare https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/202 and https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/231
(Note, this is likely something we should fix when doing our config file rewrite for podman 6.0)
Yes; this is working as documented, and pedantically, changing that carries some risk of breaking users — _exactly_ users with non-default setups like you.
Short-term, most tools tend to have CLI options that let users explicitly choose a directory instead of relying on the local search; in this case, `podman pull --cert-dir=…`.

As a pedantic note. The default for `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is generally `$HOME/.config`. It should be noted that the creation of `.config` folder is not part of the expectation on the implementer of the XDG_CONFIG_HOME variable, only the sub-directory for it's particular configuration.

c/image should make (if it doesn't exist) the `containers` folder and then it's expected files. Otherwise we could have duplicate `.config` folders. `XDG_CONFIG_HOME=$HOME/.config` => `$HOME/.config/.config/containers` where we would want `$HOME/.config/containers`.
@Luap99 I think this happened in #746 but please double-check after me; and close if so.
